title: Sosulski et al. 2019 — Online Optimization of Stimulation Speed in an Auditory Brain-Computer Interface under Time Constraints
description: An auditory event-related potential dataset from 13 healthy subjects performing an oddball paradigm with two sinusoidal tones (target 1000 Hz, non-target 500 Hz) presented at variable stimulus onset asynchronies (60-600 ms). The dataset comprises 31-channel EEG recordings at 1000 Hz acquired with BrainProducts BrainAmp DC hardware, designed to evaluate Bayesian optimization strategies for automated selection of individually optimal stimulation parameters in brain-computer interface applications.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 13 healthy subjects (mean age 22.7±1.64 years) using a 31-channel BrainProducts BrainAmp DC system at 1000 Hz sampling rate with nose reference and passive Ag/AgCl sensors in standard 10-20 montage. Subjects performed an auditory oddball task with target (1000 Hz) and non-target (500 Hz) tones presented at variable SOAs. The experiment consisted of an optimization phase (four strategies, 20 minutes each) and validation phase (20 trials per SOA). Features were extracted as mean amplitudes in five time intervals (100-500 ms) across all channels, and classification was performed using regularized linear discriminant analysis with 13-fold cross-validation.
